title: On-Demand KRS Assessment — Frontend Tasks
description: When a user searches for a KRS number, the UI should check readiness and show a real-time pipeline progress indicator if data needs to be fetched/computed. The backend exposes a job-based API with polling.

response_404.description: KRS not found upstream (entity does not exist in RDF)

body.stage: checking_data | discovering_documents | downloading | etl_ingesting | computing_features | scoring

tasks.title: Add KRS search readiness check on search submit
tasks.description: When the user submits a KRS search (existing search bar), call POST /api/assessment/{krs} BEFORE navigating to the predictions page. This single call either returns 200 (data ready, proceed to predictions view) or 202 (pipeline started, show progress UI).
acceptance_criteria: On search submit, POST to /api/assessment/{krs}
If 200: navigate directly to /predictions/{krs} as today
If 202: show the pipeline progress component (FE-002) with the returned job_id
If 404: show 'KRS not found' inline error in the search bar

tasks.title: Build pipeline progress component
tasks.description: A step-indicator component that shows the user where the backend is in the assessment pipeline. Shows 6 stages as a vertical stepper or horizontal progress bar. Polls GET /api/assessment/jobs/{job_id} every 2 seconds.
acceptance_criteria: Shows all 6 stages with current stage highlighted/animated
Past stages show green checkmark
Current stage shows spinner/pulse animation
Future stages are grayed out
During 'downloading' stage, show document counter (e.g. '3/5 documents downloaded') from progress.documents_downloaded / progress.documents_total
Poll every 2 seconds using setInterval or react-query refetchInterval
Stop polling when status is 'completed' or 'failed'
On 'completed': auto-navigate to /predictions/{krs} after 1s delay
On 'failed': show error_message with a 'Retry' button that re-triggers POST /api/assessment/{krs}

tasks.title: Add loading skeleton to predictions page
tasks.description: If the user navigates directly to /predictions/{krs} (e.g. bookmark) and there's no data, show a CTA to trigger the assessment pipeline instead of just a 404.
acceptance_criteria: If GET /api/predictions/{krs} returns 404, show a card: 'No predictions available for this company yet. Click to analyze.' with a button
Button triggers POST /api/assessment/{krs} and switches to the pipeline progress component (FE-002)
If predictions exist but are stale (scored_at > 30 days ago), show a subtle 'Refresh data' link that triggers the same flow

tasks.title: Handle concurrent assessment requests gracefully
tasks.description: If a pipeline is already running for a given KRS (another user or tab triggered it), the backend returns 202 with the existing job_id. The frontend should handle this seamlessly.
acceptance_criteria: If POST /api/assessment/{krs} returns 202 with an already-running job_id, attach to that job's progress (same polling flow)
Show a subtle note: 'Analysis already in progress' so the user knows they didn't trigger a duplicate
If the job was started by another user, this is transparent — same UX

tasks.title: Add assessment status to KRS search results list
tasks.description: When showing search results or the user's KRS access list, indicate which companies have predictions ready vs need processing.
acceptance_criteria: In the KRS list/dashboard, show a status badge per KRS: 'Ready' (green), 'Processing' (yellow spinner), 'No data' (gray)
Badge state derived from data_summary in the assessment response or from existing predictions data
Clicking a 'No data' badge triggers the assessment flow
